heading: Exceptions; waivers; implementation
label: 8565 Exceptions; waivers; implementation
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Masih Alinejad Harassment and Unlawful Targeting Act of 2022, also known as the Masih Alinejad HUNT Act of 2022, and not as part of the Comprehensive Iran Sanctions, Accountability, and Divestment Act of 2010 which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Exception for intelligence, law enforcement, and national security activities
children.label: 1 Exception for intelligence, law enforcement, and national security activities
children.text: Sanctions under sections 8563 and 8564 of this title shall not apply to any authorized intelligence, law enforcement, or national security activities of the United States.

children.heading: Exception to comply with United Nations Headquarters agreement
children.label: 2 Exception to comply with United Nations Headquarters agreement
children.text: Sanctions under shall not apply with respect to the admission of an individual to the United States if the admission of the individual is necessary to permit the United States to comply with the Agreement regarding the Headquarters of the United Nations, signed at Lake Success , and entered into force , between the United Nations and the United States, the Convention on Consular Relations, done at Vienna , and entered into force , or other applicable international obligations.section 8563(c)(2) of this titleJune 26, 1947November 21, 1947April 24, 1963March 19, 1967
determines that the waiver is in the national interests of the United States; and
submits to the appropriate congressional committees a report on the waiver and the reasons for the waiver.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 1 Implementation
children.text: The President may exercise all authorities provided to the President under sections 1702 and 1704 of title 50 to carry out this subchapter.

children.heading: Penalties
children.label: 2 Penalties
children.text: A person that violates, attempts to violate, conspires to violate, or causes a violation of section 8563(c)(1) or 8564(b) of this title or any regulation, license, or order issued to carry out either such section shall be subject to the penalties set forth in subsections (b) and (c) of to the same extent as a person that commits an unlawful act described in subsection (a) of that section.section 1705 of title 50
